subject: An ornate golden brazier or incense vessel emitting curling smoke, set against a marble columned interior; visible illustration is a small horizontal panel above the saga's chapter rules text
description: The art panel shows a ceremonial golden vessel — a footed brazier or thymiaterion — placed on a stepped pedestal between two pale marble columns. Tendrils of smoke rise and curl from the bowl, suggesting an oracular or divinatory rite. The architecture reads as classical Greek temple interior, in keeping with Theros's Mediterranean-mythic visual idiom. Palette is warm golds and ambers in the foreground vessel against cooler stone-blues and grays in the background columns. The Saga frame dominates the card, with chapter symbols I-IV running down the left side; the actual illustration is a narrow letterboxed strip rather than a full-bleed scene.
foreground: Ornate golden brazier on a stepped pedestal, smoke curling upward
background: Pale marble columns and temple interior in muted blue-gray tones
